rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical metal-free 1,1-difunctionalization of gem-difluoroalkenes with two nucleophiles (alcohols/H2O and azoles) to give alpha-difluoro(alkoxyl/azolated) methylated ethers
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the aryl gem-difluoroalkene to a radical cation; cathode reduces protons to H2 (no sacrificial oxidant needed)

key_intermediates.name: alpha-alkoxy/azolyl benzyl radical (II) after Nu1H addition, deprotonation and intramolecular electron shift
alpha-alkoxy/azolyl benzyl carbocation (III) from second anodic SET

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the gem-difluoroalkene to a radical cation, which is attacked by Nu1-H; loss of a proton and intramolecular electron shift gives a benzyl radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a benzyl carbocation that is trapped by Nu2-H. Protons released are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: controlled-potential electrolysis at sub-oxidation potentials (1.25 V vs SCE, below 1.30 V onset)
probe_reagents.observation: no product at 1.25 V; 15% yield at 1.35 V, confirming need for anodic oxidation of the alkene

probe_reagents.name: 1H-imidazole / O2 trap (radical capture as hydroperoxide)
probe_reagents.observation: alpha-imidazolyl benzyl hydroperoxide/alcohol detected by GC-MS (23%), supporting a benzyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: biphenyl-type homo-coupling product observed
probe_reagents.observation: detection of (alpha-functionalized)-biphenyl species by GC-MS, consistent with benzyl radical II

notes: DFT shows HOMO of the aryl gem-difluoroalkene is delocalized over the whole molecule (not just the olefin), rationalizing anodic SET. Mechanism is proposed as radical-cation / radical-polar crossover with two sequential anodic SET events; cathodic H2 evolution removes the need for a chemical oxidant. No KIE or TEMPO-trap experiments reported.
